events.description: Join a warm and understanding space where you can share, listen, and heal. Led by Dr. Barry Goldstein, this grief support circle is all about connecting with others who get what you’re going through. Come as you are, no judgments—just open hearts and support to navigate the ups and downs of loss side by side. Everyone is welcome!

events.title: Altadena Libraries’ Mobile Library
events.description: Altadena Libraries’ Curiosity Connection Vol. 2 is rolling into Kidspace! The mobile library will be parked outside the museum every Friday from 1:30 to 4:30 pm! Sign up for a library card, check out (or return) books, grab some fun library swag, and cozy up in our outdoor reading nook. Bonus: open a Library Card and get $3 off Kidspace admission for up to 4

events.title: Queering the Archive: Mapping Queer Pasts & Presents
events.description: How can maps reveal, obscure, or reimagine queer histories? In this workshop, we’ll explore creative approaches to mapping through examples from the Wende Museum’s collections and queer archival practices. Using personal memories and archival materials, we’ll create our own maps connecting history with lived experience. Mapping Queer Pasts & Presents is one
events.venue: Glorya Kaufman Community Center at the Wende Museum

events.title: Celebrating Pride
events.description: Celebrate Pride Diversity makes our community vibrant, and we welcome all types of families to play and learn at Kidspace! Join us for a community art project to celebrate this diversity and recognize LGBTQ+ families during Pride month. Everyone is invited to represent their unique family by decorating a family flag. events.title: Queering the Archive: Alternative Queer Poetics
events.description: What happens when the archive is incomplete? This workshop explores poetry to engage with gaps, silences, and traces in the historical record. Working with a selection of Wende Museum archival photographs, we’ll experiment with poetry’s potential to imagine perspectives and possibilities beyond the frame. Alternative Queer Poetics is one part of a three-part
events.venue: Glorya Kaufman Community Center at the Wende Museum

events.title: Queering the Archive: Cut Copy Queer Paste
events.description: What makes collage a queer medium? Throughout this hands-on session, we’ll cut, layer, juxtapose, and reinterpret pre-existing narratives. Using the Wende Museum’s collection as inspiration, we’ll construct collages and experiment with conversations and frictions between words and images. Cut Copy Queer Paste is one part of a three-part workshop series, Quee
events.venue: Glorya Kaufman Community Center at the Wende Museum

events.title: The Battle of Algiers | Cinema Under Pressure
events.description: Across continents and decades, filmmakers have faced censorship, intimidation, and exile for telling uncomfortable truths. Some films were banned outright. Others were attacked, suppressed, or quietly buried. The Battle of Algiers is a perfect example of the tension between creative expression and state control. Set during the Algerian War of Independence, t
